title: olearia quinquevulnera
intro: Olearia quinquevulnera, also known as the Five Wounds Daisy Bush, is a shrubby plant native to Australia, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Olearia quinquevulnera include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource for treating minor cuts and scrapes. Herbal preparations of Olearia quinquevulnera can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea that can be applied topically or taken internally.
